description: Canonical Codex model-profile routing policy. Logical profiles map to concrete Codex CLI model IDs and reasoning-effort values. This file is the sole source of truth for model selection -- three-brain.md documents the routing rules in prose, scripts/ops/model-routing.ts and scripts/ops/codex-exec.ts are the only code paths permitted to resolve or validate a profile, and no other file may hardcode a concrete Codex model ID.

verified_against.verification_method: codex --version / codex exec --help / ~/.codex/models_cache.json model catalog inspected directly on the installed CLI -- concrete model slugs and supported_reasoning_levels are copied from that catalog, not guessed.

codex-terra-medium.description: Balanced agentic coding model for everyday work. Default profile for normal clear-scope T2 Codex lanes.

codex-sol-high.description: Frontier agentic coding model. Used for complex or higher-stakes Codex lanes that still pass through the full Rule 1 / Rule 6 guardrail chain in three-brain.md (Claude critique, runtime verification, human review before merge). This profile does not itself grant T1 merge authority or bypass any existing gate.

codex-sol-max.description: Maximum reasoning depth. Reserved for a stuck lane that has already failed under codex-sol-high, or an explicitly PM-authorized escalation. Mechanically UNAVAILABLE (enabled: false) as of PM review on UTV2-1526 (2026-07-13): a caller-supplied authorized_by/reason override is not proof of PM authorization -- it is self-asserted, the same loophole UTV2-1521 already closed for file-scope overrides. scripts/ops/model-routing.ts#resolveModelProfile rejects any requires_pm_authorization:true profile unconditionally, regardless of override content, as defense in depth beyond this enabled flag. Re-enabling this profile requires a trusted external authorization mechanism (e.g. an authenticated PR-comment scheme mirroring docs/05_operations/schemas/scope-override-v1.md, verified against CODEOWNERS) landing in a follow-up governance lane -- do not flip enabled:true without it.

codex-luna-low.description: Fast and affordable agentic coding model. Defined for future use but reserved/disabled -- no ratified use case exists yet. Resolution must fail closed with profile_disabled while enabled=false. Do not enable to manufacture work for this profile; enabling requires a separate ratified policy change.

legacy_compatibility.description: The real compatibility boundary is the lane manifest's schema_version field (1 vs 2), not model_routing's presence or absence -- presence alone cannot distinguish 'predates this field' from 'was deleted from a schema_version-2 manifest' (PM review finding #2 on UTV2-1526). schema_version-1 manifests (created by ops:lane-start before this policy shipped) may lack model_routing; scripts/ops/codex-exec.ts resolves default_profile for those, at execution time only, and never writes it back into the historical manifest -- it is recorded solely in that run's evidence output (legacy_compatibility_used: true) and printed as a visible stderr warning. A schema_version-2 Codex-executor manifest MUST have model_routing; scripts/ops/shared.ts#validateManifest and codex-exec.ts#resolveExecModelRouting both reject one that is missing it, deleted or not.
